title: Genome Assembler Knowledge Graph
description: A curated map of genome assembly tools in terms of inputs, algorithmic primitives, functional modules, and pipeline steps.

nodes.label: De novo assembly
nodes.summary: Reference-free genome reconstruction.

nodes.label: Diploid phasing
nodes.summary: Separate maternal and paternal haplotypes.

nodes.label: Near T2T completeness
nodes.summary: Push through centromeres, segmental duplications, and telomeres.

nodes.label: Repeat resolution
nodes.summary: Disentangle large and near-identical repeats.

nodes.label: Giant genome assembly
nodes.summary: Assemble repeat-saturated 20-40+ Gb genomes that are usually impractical without HPC-scale compute.

nodes.label: Assembly validation
nodes.summary: Measure consensus quality, completeness, structural soundness, and contamination rather than relying on contig length alone.

nodes.label: Structural accuracy
nodes.summary: Minimize chimeric joins, inversions, relocations, false gaps, and other chromosome-scale structural errors.

nodes.label: Contamination control
nodes.summary: Separate target-genome sequence from cobiont, laboratory, or environmental DNA before downstream interpretation.

nodes.label: High-heterozygosity assembly
nodes.summary: Resolve divergent haplotypes without collapsing alleles or duplicating them into false primary contigs.

nodes.label: Polyploid assembly
nodes.summary: Separate homologous chromosome sets in auto- or allopolyploid genomes where chromosome-scale phasing is intrinsically difficult.

nodes.label: Chromosome scaffolding
nodes.summary: Order and orient contigs into chromosome-scale scaffolds.

nodes.label: Reference-guided improvement
nodes.summary: Improve an assembly by ordering, orienting, or patching against a reference.

nodes.label: Consensus finishing
nodes.summary: Improve base accuracy and close gaps after draft assembly.

nodes.label: Pangenome graph construction
nodes.summary: Integrate multiple high-quality assemblies into a shared graph representation.

nodes.label: Draft scaffolding
nodes.summary: Improve fragmented draft contigs into longer scaffold structures.

nodes.label: Hybrid assembly
nodes.summary: Combine short and long reads to improve continuity and accuracy.

nodes.label: Allelic redundancy reduction
nodes.summary: Remove redundant alternative contigs and recover a cleaner primary assembly.

nodes.label: Metagenome assembly
nodes.summary: Assemble mixed-community sequencing data despite uneven abundance, repeats, and strain variation.

nodes.label: Organelle genome assembly
nodes.summary: Recover plastid or mitochondrial genomes from whole-genome or genome-skimming reads.

nodes.label: Metagenome assembly evaluation
nodes.summary: Benchmark metagenome assemblies against close references while accounting for mixed-species and interspecies chimeras.

nodes.label: Single-cell genome assembly
nodes.summary: Assemble microbial genomes from MDA-amplified single-cell sequencing data despite extreme coverage bias and chimera formation.

nodes.label: Mini-metagenome assembly
nodes.summary: Assemble low-complexity pools of MDA-amplified cells that sit between single-cell and full metagenome regimes.

nodes.label: Plastome assembly
nodes.summary: Recover and structurally validate chloroplast genomes, including inverted-repeat boundaries and the canonical quadripartite layout.

nodes.label: MAG recovery
nodes.summary: Partition metagenome assemblies into metagenome-assembled genomes and refine them into a cleaner non-redundant set.

nodes.label: MAG quality evaluation
nodes.summary: Estimate completeness and contamination of recovered bins using lineage-aware marker sets.

nodes.label: Genome taxonomy placement
nodes.summary: Place recovered genomes into a curated reference taxonomy so MAGs can be compared consistently across studies.

nodes.label: Transcript-guided scaffolding
nodes.summary: Use RNA-seq or transcript alignments to connect contigs across genes and improve both scaffold continuity and gene models.

nodes.label: Assembly graph inspection
nodes.summary: Inspect assembly or pangenome graphs directly rather than relying only on flattened contigs or scaffolds.

nodes.label: Viral genome recovery
nodes.summary: Recover viral genomes or UViGs from metagenomes or viromes where viral sequence is sparse, diverse, and often fragmented.

nodes.label: Viral genome quality evaluation
nodes.summary: Estimate viral completeness, identify closed genomes, and remove host contamination from proviruses and viral contigs.

nodes.label: MAG curation
nodes.summary: Refine draft bins into cleaner, less chimeric, and more interpretable genome sets with automated and manual curation.

nodes.label: Eukaryotic MAG evaluation
nodes.summary: Estimate completeness and contamination for eukaryotic genomes recovered from metagenomic assemblies using eukaryote-aware marker sets.

nodes.label: Haploid
nodes.summary: A haploid or nearly haploid sample removes allelic ambiguity and usually simplifies assembly graph structure.

nodes.label: Diploid
nodes.summary: Two homologous chromosome sets create bubbles and phasing problems that assemblers must separate or collapse carefully.

nodes.label: Polyploid
nodes.summary: Multiple related chromosome sets amplify homoeolog confusion and make chromosome-scale phasing especially difficult.

nodes.label: High heterozygosity
nodes.summary: Divergent haplotypes inflate bubbles, alternate paths, and duplicated primary contigs if assembly is not haplotype aware.

nodes.label: Segmental duplications
nodes.summary: Long, near-identical duplicated sequence blocks are a major barrier to complete and structurally correct assembly.

nodes.label: Recent whole-genome duplication
nodes.summary: Recent WGD leaves large blocks of highly similar duplicated sequence that are difficult to separate cleanly.

nodes.label: High repeat content
nodes.summary: Repeat-dense genomes create graph ambiguity, collapse risk, and strong dependence on very long-range sequence context.

nodes.label: High GC / compositional bias
nodes.summary: Composition bias can distort coverage and complicate interpretation of read support and contamination profiles.

nodes.label: Ultra-large introns
nodes.summary: Gene loci with extremely large introns enlarge the genomic search space around genes and complicate locus-scale continuity.

nodes.label: Organelle contamination / carryover
nodes.summary: Mitochondrial, plastid, or other non-target contigs can persist in a draft assembly unless screened explicitly.

nodes.label: Microbial genome
nodes.summary: Compact microbial genomes where polishing and circular finishing are common priorities.

nodes.label: Uneven abundance / coverage
nodes.summary: Mixed-community samples often exhibit highly skewed abundance distributions across taxa.

nodes.label: Strain heterogeneity
nodes.summary: Closely related strains create bubbles, local ambiguity, and graph fragmentation in metagenomic assembly.

nodes.label: Small circular genome target
nodes.summary: Organelle assemblies often aim to recover compact circular molecules from a larger sequencing background.

nodes.label: Low-abundance taxa
nodes.summary: Rare members of a community can be missed or fragmented without methods that exploit long-range or hybrid evidence.

nodes.label: MDA amplification bias
nodes.summary: Multiple displacement amplification creates extreme depth variability across loci in single-cell datasets.

nodes.label: Chimeric MDA reads / pairs
nodes.summary: MDA introduces chimeric reads and read pairs that create false edges and local graph confusion.

nodes.label: PacBio HiFi
nodes.summary: Accurate long reads that changed the design space for assemblers.

nodes.label: ONT simplex
nodes.summary: Long nanopore reads with improving base accuracy and strong span length.

nodes.label: PacBio CLR / noisy long reads
nodes.summary: Older noisy long reads that reward aggressive repeat-aware graph handling.

nodes.label: Illumina short-read WGS
nodes.summary: High-coverage short-read whole-genome shotgun data used in older conifer and megagenome assembly stacks.

nodes.label: ONT long + ultra-long
nodes.summary: Nanopore long-read regime used to span extreme repeats in the largest animal genomes.

nodes.label: Illumina paired-end
nodes.summary: Accurate short reads still widely used for k-mer validation, contamination screening, and orthogonal polishing or benchmarking.

nodes.label: ONT ultra-long
nodes.summary: Supplementary ultra-long reads used to bridge very large repeats.

nodes.label: Hi-C
nodes.summary: Long-range contact data used for scaffolding and phasing.

nodes.label: Trio / parental data
nodes.summary: Parental markers used to partition or phase diploid assemblies.

nodes.label: Strand-seq
nodes.summary: Chromosome-scale directional data used in difficult phasing workflows.

nodes.label: High-memory HPC / cloud
nodes.summary: Large-memory, high-I/O infrastructure that giant-genome assembly usually requires; not a realistic local-laptop assumption.

nodes.label: Dense linkage / SNP map
nodes.summary: Marker-dense genetic maps used to order and orient giant-genome scaffolds into chromosomes.

nodes.label: Fosmid / clone pools
nodes.summary: Clone-pool sequencing used to break giant genomes into tractable subassemblies and merging constraints.

nodes.label: Haploid tissue / megagametophyte
nodes.summary: Haploid tissue that suppresses heterozygosity and made several conifer megagenomes more tractable.

nodes.label: PacBio long reads
nodes.summary: Supplementary long reads used to upgrade short-read megagenome drafts through mega-read construction.

nodes.label: 10x linked reads
nodes.summary: Barcoded linked-read molecules can extend phasing and scaffolding context beyond ordinary short-read insert sizes.

nodes.label: Bionano optical maps
nodes.summary: Genome maps provide long-range structural evidence for scaffold validation, correction, and chromosome-scale organization.

nodes.label: RNA-seq / transcript evidence
nodes.summary: Transcript evidence can expose missing or fragmented genic regions and provide an orthogonal sanity check on assembly completeness.

nodes.label: Reference genome / synteny
nodes.summary: A related or benchmark reference enables alignment-based detection of inversions, relocations, translocations, and local misjoins.

nodes.label: Taxonomic / GC / coverage profile
nodes.summary: Joint taxonomic assignments, GC composition, and coverage profiles help isolate non-target contigs and mixed-origin assemblies.

nodes.label: Gamete / pollen sequencing
nodes.summary: Single-gamete data can reinforce haplotype assignment in polyploid phasing and chromosome reconstruction.

nodes.label: Mate-pair libraries
nodes.summary: Long-insert short-read pairs provide older-generation scaffold linkage beyond ordinary paired-end distances.

nodes.label: ONT duplex
nodes.summary: Duplex nanopore reads raise per-read accuracy and can improve consensus quality and diploid assembly performance.

nodes.label: BAC / physical map
nodes.summary: Large-insert clone maps used in hierarchical shotgun sequencing.

nodes.label: Homologous reference genomes
nodes.summary: Closely related reference genomes used for homology-guided polishing.

nodes.label: ONT raw signal / FAST5
nodes.summary: Signal-level ONT data required by some consensus polishers.

nodes.label: Multiple high-quality assemblies
nodes.summary: Set of phased or near-complete assemblies used to induce a pangenome graph.

nodes.label: Fragmented draft assembly
nodes.summary: Preassembled contigs or scaffolds that need scaffolding, curation, or deduplication.

nodes.label: Read-depth alignment profile
nodes.summary: Coverage and alignment profile used to identify redundant or haplotig contigs.

nodes.label: Genome skimming data
nodes.summary: Low- to moderate-depth whole-genome reads can be enriched enough in organelle sequence to support targeted recovery.

nodes.label: Seed sequence / bait reference
nodes.summary: A conserved organelle gene or bait sequence can recruit target reads and start iterative extension.

nodes.label: Read clouds / barcoded partitions
nodes.summary: Barcode-partitioned short reads preserve long-range context for subassembly and repeat resolution in mixed communities.

nodes.label: MDA-amplified single-cell DNA
nodes.summary: Whole-genome amplified DNA from a single cell is the dominant input regime for classical microbial single-cell assembly.

nodes.label: Mini-metagenome pooled cells
nodes.summary: A small pool of random cells creates a lower-complexity community than a full metagenome while preserving MDA artifacts.

nodes.label: Reference plastome panel
nodes.summary: A curated plastid reference collection can recruit chloroplast reads and help orient the finished plastome structure.

nodes.label: Multisample coverage profiles
nodes.summary: Coverage covariance across samples helps separate genomes that overlap in composition but differ in abundance dynamics.

nodes.label: Lineage marker gene sets
nodes.summary: Single-copy marker gene collections drive both marker-guided binning and lineage-aware MAG quality assessment.

nodes.label: GTDB reference taxonomy
nodes.summary: A curated Genome Taxonomy Database reference set anchors phylogenomic placement and standardized naming.

nodes.label: Recovered bin set
nodes.summary: Candidate bins or MAG drafts carried forward into refinement, quality control, and taxonomic placement.

nodes.label: Pairwise assembly alignments
nodes.summary: All-vs-all homology mappings between assemblies that can be induced into a pangenome graph.

nodes.label: GFA graph file
nodes.summary: Sequence-graph interchange file used for graph inspection, smoothing, and downstream graph-native analysis.

nodes.label: Metavirome / viral-enriched library
nodes.summary: Viral-enriched sequencing data can increase the share of viral reads and improve recovery of complete viral genomes.

nodes.label: Candidate viral contigs
nodes.summary: Contig set suspected to be viral, awaiting filtering, QC, binning, or completeness estimation.

nodes.label: Viral hallmark HMM panel
nodes.summary: Curated viral hallmark models help distinguish viral contigs from cellular background sequence.

nodes.label: Reference viral genome database
nodes.summary: Reference viral genomes and proteins support completeness estimation and host-contamination trimming.

nodes.label: Eukaryotic marker gene sets
nodes.summary: Eukaryote-aware marker sets are needed to assess completeness and contamination of eukaryotic MAGs.

nodes.label: OLC / string graph
nodes.summary: Overlap-layout-consensus family for long-read assembly.

nodes.label: Phased assembly graph
nodes.summary: Graph construction specialized for haplotype separation with accurate long reads.

nodes.label: Multiplex de Bruijn graph
nodes.summary: Long-read de Bruijn approach with variable k across the graph.

nodes.label: Double graph
nodes.summary: Hybrid graph design that pairs accurate long reads with ultra-long support.

nodes.label: Repeat graph
nodes.summary: Graph simplification built around repeat structure in noisy long-read assembly.

nodes.label: Run-length / marker graph
nodes.summary: Compressed marker representation optimized for ONT assembly speed.

nodes.label: Homopolymer-compressed OLC
nodes.summary: Overlap assembly tuned to HiFi homopolymer error modes.

nodes.label: ONT-aware assembly graph
nodes.summary: Assembly graph design specialized for modern ONT simplex reads.

nodes.label: Anchored scaffold integration
nodes.summary: Integrate preassembled giant-genome scaffolds with linkage or Hi-C evidence to produce chromosome-scale structure.

nodes.label: Pool-merged short-read hybrid
nodes.summary: Short-read giant-genome strategy that merges WGS and pooled-clone assemblies under strong compute constraints.

nodes.label: Super-read / mega-read hybrid
nodes.summary: Compress short reads into super-reads and optionally long-read-informed mega-reads before giant-genome assembly.

nodes.label: Trio binning
nodes.summary: Partition offspring long reads by parental markers before assembly to simplify diploid haplotype reconstruction.

nodes.label: k-mer spectrum validation
nodes.summary: Use k-mer spectra from accurate reads to estimate consensus QV, completeness, copy-number balance, and phasing quality.

nodes.label: Single-copy ortholog benchmarking
nodes.summary: Assess assembly or annotation completeness by checking conserved single-copy ortholog sets expected for the lineage.

nodes.label: Reference-alignment QC
nodes.summary: Compare an assembly against a reference to expose large-scale misassemblies and contiguity-versus-correctness tradeoffs.

nodes.label: Contaminant binning
nodes.summary: Separate contigs by taxonomic origin, GC content, and coverage signal to identify non-target sequence.

nodes.label: Haplotig / overlap purging
nodes.summary: Use depth and alignments to remove redundant haplotigs and uncollapsed overlaps from a nominal primary assembly.

nodes.label: LTR-based repeat completeness
nodes.summary: Estimate repeat-space assembly quality from intact versus fragmented long terminal repeat retrotransposons and the LAI score.

nodes.label: Allele-aware polyploid scaffolding
nodes.summary: Use Hi-C and related signals to separate homologous chromosomes and scaffold polyploid assemblies without collapsing alleles.

nodes.label: Minimizer overlap-layout
nodes.summary: Rapid overlap-layout assembly driven by minimizer-based mapping.

nodes.label: Fuzzy de Bruijn graph
nodes.summary: Long-read assembly using a fuzzy de Bruijn graph formulation.

nodes.label: Hi-C contact scaffolding
nodes.summary: Use Hi-C contact frequency to order and orient contigs into chromosomes.

nodes.label: Reference-guided scaffolding
nodes.summary: Use a related reference to scaffold or patch a draft assembly.

nodes.label: Consensus polishing
nodes.summary: Refine assembled sequence with aligned reads to improve consensus accuracy.

nodes.label: Gap bridging
nodes.summary: Bridge scaffold gaps using long reads and local consensus.

nodes.label: Assembly reconciliation
nodes.summary: Merge complementary assemblies into a single improved result.

nodes.label: Hierarchical shotgun sequencing
nodes.summary: Clone-by-clone assembly strategy built on a physical map and tiling path.

nodes.label: Hi-C contact map processing
nodes.summary: Transform raw Hi-C reads into normalized contact maps for inspection and scaffolding.

nodes.label: Hi-C misjoin correction
nodes.summary: Use contact-map discontinuities to detect and break misassemblies before scaffolding.

nodes.label: Neural consensus polishing
nodes.summary: Neural-network polishing of draft assemblies from basecalled ONT reads.

nodes.label: Homologous polishing
nodes.summary: Use related genomes to identify and correct systematic consensus errors.

nodes.label: Bloom-filter k-mer polishing
nodes.summary: Use memory-efficient k-mer evidence to correct assembly bases.

nodes.label: Assembly-derived pangenome graph
nodes.summary: Construct a graph reference directly from high-quality assemblies.

nodes.label: Signal-level polishing
nodes.summary: Improve consensus with signal-level ONT evidence rather than only basecalled reads.

nodes.label: Long-read scaffolding
nodes.summary: Use long noisy reads to link and order preassembled contigs.

nodes.label: Paired-link scaffolding
nodes.summary: Use paired-end or mate-pair insert constraints to scaffold fragmented assemblies.

nodes.label: Bloom-filter gap closing
nodes.summary: Close scaffold gaps by traversing a Bloom-filter de Bruijn graph.

nodes.label: Exact repeat-aware scaffolding
nodes.summary: Search scaffold solutions with performance guarantees under repeat-aware constraints.

nodes.label: Alignment-free long-read scaffolding
nodes.summary: Link contigs using long-read k-mer pairs without full alignments.

nodes.label: Linked-read k-mer scaffolding
nodes.summary: Use linked-read barcodes or kmers to build chromosome-scale scaffolds.

nodes.label: Streaming scaffolding
nodes.summary: Continuously update scaffolds while long-read data streams from the sequencer.

nodes.label: Hybrid DBG-to-OLC assembly
nodes.summary: Convert short-read contigs into a compact long-read assembly backbone.

nodes.label: Hybrid graph bridging
nodes.summary: Resolve short-read assembly graphs with long-read bridges.

nodes.label: Haplotype-aware polishing
nodes.summary: Polish and phase long-read assemblies with haplotype-aware models.

nodes.label: Pangenome graph analytics
nodes.summary: Analyze, transform, and lay out pangenome graphs after construction.

nodes.label: Multi-reference scaffolding
nodes.summary: Scaffold assemblies using multiple related references and synteny structure.

nodes.label: Long-read assembly upgrade
nodes.summary: Upgrade draft contigs using long reads after an initial assembly is complete.

nodes.label: Linked-read misassembly correction
nodes.summary: Use linked-read molecule support to detect and cut likely misassemblies.

nodes.label: Graph affix collapse
nodes.summary: Collapse walk-preserving shared affixes to reduce graph redundancy.

nodes.label: Succinct metagenome de Bruijn graph
nodes.summary: Use succinct de Bruijn graph construction to assemble very large metagenomic short-read datasets efficiently.

nodes.label: Strain-aware metagenome graph assembly
nodes.summary: Build and simplify assembly graphs while preserving signal from closely related strains in a community.

nodes.label: Long-read metagenome repeat graph
nodes.summary: Extend repeat-graph assembly ideas to uneven, strain-rich long-read metagenomic datasets.

nodes.label: Seeded organelle read recruitment
nodes.summary: Recruit organelle reads iteratively from whole-genome data using seed sequences and graph-aware extension.

nodes.label: Seed-and-extend organelle assembly
nodes.summary: Assemble small circular organelle genomes by extending outward from a conserved seed sequence.

nodes.label: Mitogenome recovery and packaging
nodes.summary: Recover mitochondrial scaffolds from short reads and package them into an annotated organelle result.

nodes.label: Hybrid metagenome clustering and scaffolding
nodes.summary: Cluster metagenome contigs into species- or strain-level partitions and scaffold them with long-read evidence.

nodes.label: Graph-motif metagenome scaffolding
nodes.summary: Use assembly-graph motifs to scaffold contigs and interpret biological variants in metagenomic datasets.

nodes.label: Read-cloud subassembly
nodes.summary: Use barcode-linked short reads to build local subassemblies that bridge repeats and improve microbial draft contiguity.

nodes.label: Reference-guided metagenome benchmarking
nodes.summary: Evaluate mixed-community assemblies against multiple close references while detecting metagenome-specific structural errors.

nodes.label: MDA-aware de Bruijn graph assembly
nodes.summary: Assemble single-cell reads with graph logic adapted to chimeric MDA products and highly nonuniform coverage.

nodes.label: Uneven-depth de Bruijn graph assembly
nodes.summary: Adapt graph assembly to depth variability using local assembly and depth-relative filtering instead of even-coverage assumptions.

nodes.label: Mini-metagenome pool assembly
nodes.summary: Treat a pooled-cell mini-metagenome as a lower-complexity assembly target while preserving single-cell-style artifact handling.

nodes.label: Plastome structural finishing
nodes.summary: Resolve plastome inverted-repeat boundaries and orient the assembly into a canonical chloroplast structure.

nodes.label: Composition + coverage binning
nodes.summary: Group contigs by combining composition signatures with differential abundance or depth patterns across samples.

nodes.label: Marker-guided binning
nodes.summary: Use lineage markers and abundance patterns to seed or refine metagenome bins.

nodes.label: Consensus bin refinement
nodes.summary: Compare multiple binner outputs and score candidate bins to keep a cleaner non-redundant set.

nodes.label: Marker-gene MAG QC
nodes.summary: Estimate MAG completeness and contamination using lineage-aware single-copy marker genes.

nodes.label: Phylogenomic genome placement
nodes.summary: Place recovered genomes into a curated phylogenomic taxonomy using reference markers and trees.

nodes.label: Transcript-guided scaffolding
nodes.summary: Infer scaffold joins from spliced transcript evidence or RNA-seq read pairs aligned across fragmented assemblies.

nodes.label: Interactive graph visualization
nodes.summary: Lay out and inspect graph structure directly so repeats, bubbles, and alternate paths can be interpreted visually.

nodes.label: Viral assembly-graph recovery
nodes.summary: Use assembly-graph structure and coverage behavior to extract candidate viral subgraphs from mixed metagenomic assemblies.

nodes.label: Viral sequence classification
nodes.summary: Classify contigs as viral using hallmark genes, genome structure features, and expert-guided multi-classifier rules.

nodes.label: Viral genome QC
nodes.summary: Estimate viral completeness, identify terminal repeats or closed genomes, and trim host contamination from proviruses.

nodes.label: Viral metagenome binning
nodes.summary: Cluster viral contigs using composition and coverage to reconstruct more complete viral genomes from fragmented viromes.

nodes.label: Deep latent metagenome binning
nodes.summary: Use deep latent representations of composition and coabundance to cluster contigs into higher-quality bins.

nodes.label: Self-supervised contig binning
nodes.summary: Learn contig embeddings with self-supervised constraints to improve short- and long-read MAG recovery.

nodes.label: Interactive bin curation
nodes.summary: Use human-guided inspection of coverage, composition, and single-copy marker signals to refine metagenome bins.

nodes.label: Eukaryotic MAG QC
nodes.summary: Estimate eukaryotic MAG completeness and contamination using dynamically selected eukaryotic markers.

nodes.label: Unitig
nodes.summary: A maximal unbranched graph path that captures locally unambiguous assembly sequence before higher-order scaffolding.

nodes.label: Contig
nodes.summary: A contiguous assembled sequence without scaffold gaps.

nodes.label: Scaffold
nodes.summary: An ordered and oriented chain of contigs, often containing explicit gap placeholders.

nodes.label: Bubble
nodes.summary: A branching graph structure often caused by heterozygous sequence or local assembly ambiguity.

nodes.label: Tip
nodes.summary: A short dead-end branch in an assembly graph, often reflecting error or insufficient support.

nodes.label: Repeat collapse
nodes.summary: Multiple distinct repeat copies are merged into a single path because reads cannot separate them reliably.

nodes.label: Chimeric join
nodes.summary: A false adjacency where unrelated loci are assembled or scaffolded together.

nodes.label: Haplotig
nodes.summary: A redundant contig representing an alternate haplotype sequence that remains in a nominal primary assembly.

nodes.label: Graph tangling
nodes.summary: Dense, repeat-driven graph ambiguity with too many alternative traversals for straightforward simplification.

nodes.label: Coverage drop
nodes.summary: A local decrease in read support that can signal bias, contamination, gap risk, or assembly error.

nodes.label: Overcollapsed repeats
nodes.summary: Distinct repeat copies are over-merged, erasing copy-number or structural differences.

nodes.label: False duplication
nodes.summary: The assembly appears to duplicate sequence because haplotigs or overlaps were not purged from the primary representation.

nodes.label: Overpurging
nodes.summary: Aggressive redundancy removal deletes legitimate sequence rather than only redundant allelic copies.

nodes.label: Underphasing
nodes.summary: Phased assembly remains too mixed or too short-ranged to separate homologous chromosomes cleanly.

nodes.label: Mis-scaffolding
nodes.summary: Long-range evidence orders or orients contigs incorrectly, producing false chromosome structure.

nodes.label: Consensus drift
nodes.summary: Consensus sequence accumulates residual systematic error or deviates from the dominant true sequence.

nodes.label: Pseudomolecule
nodes.summary: Chromosome-scale sequence assembled by ordering and orienting contigs or scaffolds.

nodes.label: Assembly gap
nodes.summary: Unknown sequence represented between contigs or scaffolds.

nodes.label: Clone tiling path
nodes.summary: Ordered set of overlapping BAC clones spanning a region of the genome.

nodes.label: Hi-C contact map
nodes.summary: Matrix view of contact frequencies used for scaffolding and curation.

nodes.label: Superscaffold
nodes.summary: Large scaffold grouping generated during chromosome-scale Hi-C assembly.

nodes.label: Pangenome graph
nodes.summary: Graph representation of multiple assembled genomes and their homology relationships.

nodes.label: Variation graph
nodes.summary: Graph representation encoding many alternative genome sequences and paths.

nodes.label: Redundant contig
nodes.summary: Alternative or duplicated contig representing allelic redundancy rather than novel sequence.

nodes.label: Graph path
nodes.summary: A haplotype, assembly, or reference traversal through a pangenome graph.

nodes.label: Complete replicon
nodes.summary: Closed bacterial chromosome or plasmid recovered as a complete circular molecule.

nodes.label: Synteny block
nodes.summary: Conserved block used to infer contig order across related references.

nodes.label: Linked-read molecule span
nodes.summary: Long molecule support used to validate or break draft assemblies.

nodes.label: Shared graph affix
nodes.summary: Walk-preserving shared affix in a variation graph that can be collapsed.

nodes.label: Microdiversity / strain variation
nodes.summary: Metagenomic graphs may contain local variants caused by related strains within the same community.

nodes.label: Circular organelle genome
nodes.summary: Plastid and mitochondrial genomes are often recovered as compact circular molecules.

nodes.label: Targeted read recruitment
nodes.summary: Seed-guided selection of informative reads reduces background from non-target nuclear sequence.

nodes.label: Assembly graph motif
nodes.summary: Distinct graph structures such as bubbles and repeats can signal biological variation or problematic joins in metagenomes.

nodes.label: Interspecies misassembly
nodes.summary: A contig may incorrectly join sequence from related organisms in the same community, creating a metagenome-specific structural error.

nodes.label: Read-cloud barcode partition
nodes.summary: Barcodes partition short reads into long-range linked groups that can seed local subassemblies.

nodes.label: Strain-resolved draft genome
nodes.summary: A metagenome assembly output that preserves distinct genomes or sub-strains instead of collapsing them into one consensus.

nodes.label: Chimeric edge
nodes.summary: False de Bruijn graph edges introduced by chimeric MDA products can block long contig construction.

nodes.label: Single amplified genome (SAG)
nodes.summary: A single-cell whole-genome amplification product used as the assembly target in microbial single-cell genomics.

nodes.label: Mini-metagenome
nodes.summary: A small pooled-cell assembly regime that is less complex than bulk metagenomics but harder than single-cell assembly.

nodes.label: Coverage dropout
nodes.summary: Single-cell amplification can erase or strongly deplete regions, leaving severe low-depth gaps and branch ambiguity.

nodes.label: Inverted repeat
nodes.summary: Duplicated plastome repeat block whose boundaries are a common structural checkpoint during chloroplast assembly.

nodes.label: Quadripartite plastome
nodes.summary: Canonical plastid genome structure with large and small single-copy regions separated by inverted repeats.

nodes.label: MAG
nodes.summary: Metagenome-assembled genome recovered by binning contigs from a mixed-community assembly.

nodes.label: Contig bin
nodes.summary: A provisional set of contigs grouped as if they originate from the same genome.

nodes.label: Tetranucleotide frequency
nodes.summary: Short-word composition signature widely used with depth information to separate metagenome bins.

nodes.label: Lineage marker set
nodes.summary: Lineage-specific collection of mostly single-copy genes used to anchor binning or evaluate MAG quality.

nodes.label: Phylogenomic taxonomic placement
nodes.summary: Assignment of a recovered genome into a reference taxonomy using marker-gene-based phylogenomic placement.

nodes.label: Spliced contig bridge
nodes.summary: Transcript or spliced read evidence linking exons that land on different contigs, implying scaffold adjacency.

nodes.label: Exon block graph
nodes.summary: Graph over exon-supported contig blocks used to choose a transcript-consistent scaffold path.

nodes.label: GFA sequence graph
nodes.summary: Graph encoded in the Graphical Fragment Assembly format for assembly or pangenome inspection.

nodes.label: UViG
nodes.summary: Uncultivated viral genome recovered from metagenomic data, often used as the unit of viral discovery.

nodes.label: Provirus
nodes.summary: Viral sequence integrated into a host genome that must be separated from flanking host sequence during QC.

nodes.label: Viral hallmark gene
nodes.summary: Conserved viral gene family used to identify candidate viral sequence in contigs or graph segments.

nodes.label: Complete viral genome
nodes.summary: Recovered viral genome considered closed or sufficiently complete for higher-confidence comparative use.

nodes.label: Viral contig bin
nodes.summary: Cluster of viral contigs inferred to originate from the same viral genome or closely linked genome fragments.

nodes.label: Latent contig embedding
nodes.summary: Learned feature representation that captures contig composition and abundance structure for binning.

nodes.label: Curated MAG
nodes.summary: MAG refined manually or semi-automatically to reduce contamination, split chimeras, and improve interpretability.

nodes.label: Haplotype partitioning
nodes.summary: Separate read sets or graph paths by haplotype.

nodes.label: Long-range phasing
nodes.summary: Use Hi-C, trio, or Strand-seq signals to stabilize phasing decisions.

nodes.label: Ultra-long bridging
nodes.summary: Use very long reads to bridge large repeats and hard gaps.

nodes.label: Repeat disambiguation
nodes.summary: Collapse, split, or route through repetitive graph structure.

nodes.label: Variable-k refinement
nodes.summary: Refine graph representation with changing k to preserve long-read signal.

nodes.label: Marker compression
nodes.summary: Compress reads into marker/run-length representations before graph building.

nodes.label: Homopolymer compression
nodes.summary: Normalize homopolymer runs to simplify overlaps and repeat handling.

nodes.summary: MAG collection improved by manual or semi-automatic curation to reduce contamination and split chimeras.

nodes.label: Eukaryotic MAG quality report
nodes.summary: Eukaryote-aware completeness and contamination assessment for metagenome-recovered genomes.

nodes.label: QV
nodes.summary: Consensus quality value summarizing estimated base-level accuracy on a Phred-like scale.

nodes.label: N50
nodes.summary: Continuity statistic equal to the shortest sequence length needed to cover 50% of the assembly.

nodes.label: NG50
nodes.summary: Continuity statistic analogous to N50 but normalized to expected genome size.

nodes.label: LAI
nodes.summary: LTR Assembly Index, a repeat-space continuity score derived from intact long terminal repeat retrotransposons.

nodes.label: k-mer completeness
nodes.summary: Fraction of reliable read k-mers captured by the assembly, used as a reference-free completeness measure.

nodes.label: Switch error
nodes.summary: Frequency of haplotype switching mistakes in phased assemblies.

nodes.label: Misassembly rate
nodes.summary: Frequency of structural assembly errors such as relocations, inversions, and translocations against a benchmark.

nodes.label: Telomere recovery
nodes.summary: Extent to which chromosome ends and telomeric sequence are recovered in a near-T2T assembly.

nodes.label: Bin completeness
nodes.summary: Estimated fraction of expected lineage marker genes recovered in a bin or MAG.

nodes.label: Bin contamination
nodes.summary: Estimated redundancy of lineage marker genes suggesting mixed-origin or overmerged bins.

nodes.label: Viral completeness
nodes.summary: Estimated completeness or closure status of a viral genome recovered from metagenomic sequence.

nodes.label: CHM13
nodes.summary: Human telomere-to-telomere reference case that crystallized the modern repeat- and duplication-aware assembly agenda.

nodes.label: HG002
nodes.summary: Widely used human benchmark sample for phasing, assembly, and reference-free quality assessment.

nodes.label: Arabidopsis
nodes.summary: Compact plant-genome benchmark case frequently used for assembly and k-mer validation examples.

nodes.label: Maize
nodes.summary: Repeat-rich crop genome case where long reads and optical maps substantially improved assembly continuity.

nodes.label: Wheat
nodes.summary: Canonical very large polyploid crop-genome case with extreme duplication and chromosome-scale assembly difficulty.

nodes.label: Spruce
nodes.summary: Conifer megagenome case marked by massive repeat content and difficult short-read era assembly choices.

nodes.label: Axolotl
nodes.summary: Gigantic salamander genome case with chromosome anchoring and unusually large gene loci.

nodes.label: Lungfish

nodes.label: Human genome (2001 draft)

nodes.label: Aedes aegypti

nodes.label: HPRC draft pangenome

nodes.label: Klebsiella BAA-2146

nodes.label: Soil metagenome

nodes.label: Mock microbial community

nodes.label: Sheep gut microbiome
nodes.summary: Long-read metagenome case used to show near-complete bacterial genome recovery from a real community.

nodes.label: Plastome from genome skimming

nodes.label: Animal mitogenome
nodes.summary: Short-read mitochondrial assembly and annotation case for zoological genome projects.

nodes.label: Virtual gut microbiome

nodes.label: Antibiotic-treated gut microbiome
nodes.summary: Human gut metagenome series used to show that hybrid assembly reveals more contiguous resistome and mobile-element structure.

nodes.label: MBARC-26 mock community

nodes.label: Read-cloud gut microbiome

nodes.label: Marine sediment metagenome

nodes.label: Single-cell bacterial SAG
nodes.summary: Canonical microbial single-cell assembly regime used to benchmark uneven-depth and chimera-aware assemblers.

nodes.label: Mini-metagenome MDA pool

nodes.label: Pearl oyster draft genome

nodes.label: Rosaceae draft genomes

nodes.label: Global ocean virome

nodes.label: Human gut microbiome

nodes.label: Human skin eukaryome

nodes.label: Minimizer-space metagenome DBG
nodes.summary: Assemble accurate long-read metagenomes in minimizer space with abundance-aware filtering and iterative graph refinement.

nodes.label: Viral annotation-guided curation

nodes.label: Minimizer-space graph

nodes.label: Circular metagenome-derived genome

nodes.label: V-score

nodes.label: Minimizer-space metagenome assembly

nodes.label: Viral annotation curation

nodes.label: hifiasm-meta

nodes.label: metaMDBG

nodes.label: Mobile-element classification

nodes.label: Spliced-alignment assembly

nodes.label: Annotation consensus weighting
nodes.summary: Resolve conflicts across transcript, protein, and ab initio evidence into a single supported gene model set.

nodes.label: Colored-graph co-assembly
nodes.summary: Co-assemble related single-cell datasets while preserving library-specific support across graph paths.

nodes.label: Eukaryotic contig classification

nodes.label: Eukaryotic gene discovery

nodes.label: Sequence-to-graph alignment

nodes.label: Phables

nodes.label: iPHoP

nodes.label: PASA

nodes.label: MetaEuk

edges.rationale: Post-assembly long-read upgrading can depend on PacBio long-read support.

pipelines.label: 3D-DNA plus Juicebox curation
pipelines.tagline: Iterative Hi-C misjoin correction with manual review.
pipelines.description: 3D-DNA uses Hi-C to iteratively break misjoins and scaffold contigs, while Juicebox Assembly Tools support interactive cleanup and refinement.

pipelines.label: AGOUTI transcript-guided scaffolding
pipelines.tagline: Join contigs with RNA-seq while repairing fragmented gene models.
pipelines.description: AGOUTI uses RNA-seq evidence to scaffold fragmented assemblies and merge split gene models, making it an assembly-and-annotation bridge rather than a pure scaffolder.

pipelines.label: ALLHiC polyploid scaffolding
pipelines.tagline: Allele-aware chromosome assembly for autopolyploids.
pipelines.description: A polyploid branch that combines Hi-C and related haplotype evidence to scaffold homologous chromosomes without collapsing allelic structure.

pipelines.label: anvi'o MAG curation
pipelines.tagline: Interactively refine bins into cleaner genome sets.
pipelines.description: anvi'o supports human-guided refinement of metagenome bins using coverage, composition, and single-copy marker context to split mixed bins and improve final MAG sets.

pipelines.label: ARKS linked-read scaffolding
pipelines.tagline: Use linked-read kmers for chromosome-scale scaffolds.
pipelines.description: ARKS derives long-range scaffolding signal from linked-read kmers to improve draft human and other large-genome assemblies.

pipelines.label: Athena read-cloud metagenome assembly
pipelines.tagline: Use barcode-linked short reads for local subassembly in mixed communities.
pipelines.description: Athena leverages read clouds to perform local subassembly and improve metagenome draft quality, especially for difficult regions and lower-abundance organisms.

pipelines.label: Australian lungfish giant genome
pipelines.tagline: Nanopore plus MARVEL giant-genome path.
pipelines.description: A giant animal-genome path built from long and ultra-long Nanopore reads, MARVEL assembly, and extensive polishing before chromosome anchoring. This is emphatically not a local-computer workflow.

pipelines.label: Axolotl chromosome scaffolding (Hi-C)
pipelines.tagline: Hi-C giant-genome scaffolding path.
pipelines.description: A Hi-C-mediated axolotl path that promotes a giant salamander assembly to chromosome scale. It depends on heavy compute, long-range contact data, and a preexisting large-genome assembly substrate.

pipelines.label: Axolotl chromosome anchoring (linkage map)
pipelines.tagline: 32-Gb map-anchored giant-genome path.
pipelines.description: A chromosome-scale axolotl path built around dense genetic-map anchoring on top of preassembled giant-genome scaffolds. This is an HPC workflow, not a practical local-computer assembly job.

pipelines.label: Bandage assembly-graph inspection
pipelines.tagline: Inspect graph topology directly instead of relying only on flattened contigs.
pipelines.description: Bandage provides direct interactive inspection of sequence graphs in GFA or assembler-native graph formats, helping users see repeats, tangles, bubbles, and candidate joins.

pipelines.label: BESST paired-read scaffolding
pipelines.tagline: Probabilistic paired-link scaffold construction.
pipelines.description: BESST uses paired-end and mate-pair linkage models to scaffold large fragmented short-read assemblies.

pipelines.label: BlobToolKit contamination screen
pipelines.tagline: GC, coverage, and taxonomy for non-target sequence detection.
pipelines.description: A contamination-control path that combines accurate reads with taxonomic, GC, and coverage profiling to identify mixed-origin assemblies.

pipelines.label: BUSCO gene-space completeness
pipelines.tagline: Conserved orthologs as a completeness benchmark.
pipelines.description: A gene-content validation path that benchmarks an assembly against lineage-specific single-copy ortholog expectations.

pipelines.label: Canu on noisy long reads
pipelines.tagline: Correction-first OLC for CLR-era long reads.
pipelines.description: Canu combines raw-read correction, adaptive k-mer weighting, and repeat-aware OLC for long noisy reads.

pipelines.label: CheckM MAG quality assessment
pipelines.tagline: Estimate completeness and contamination from lineage markers.
pipelines.description: CheckM evaluates recovered bins or MAGs with lineage-aware marker genes to estimate completeness and contamination before comparative use.

pipelines.label: CheckV viral QC
pipelines.tagline: Estimate completeness and trim host contamination from viral genomes.
pipelines.description: CheckV evaluates candidate viral contigs for completeness, recognizes complete genomes, and removes host contamination from proviral sequence.

pipelines.label: Human hierarchical shotgun
pipelines.tagline: BAC-based physical mapping and clone tiling path assembly.
pipelines.description: The early human reference combined a BAC-based physical map with hierarchical shotgun sequencing along a clone tiling path.

pipelines.label: CoCoNet viral binning
pipelines.tagline: Deep-learning binning for fragmented viral metagenomes.
pipelines.description: CoCoNet clusters viral contigs with coverage and composition signals to reconstruct more coherent viral bins from fragmented viromes.

pipelines.label: CONCOCT differential-coverage binning
pipelines.tagline: Cluster contigs by composition and multisample abundance.
pipelines.description: CONCOCT groups metagenome contigs using tetranucleotide composition together with coverage profiles across multiple samples.

pipelines.label: DAS Tool MAG refinement
pipelines.tagline: Score and de-replicate bins from multiple binners.
pipelines.description: DAS Tool aggregates candidate bins from multiple binners, scores them with marker evidence, and keeps a cleaner non-redundant MAG set.

pipelines.label: DBG2OLC hybrid assembly
pipelines.tagline: Short-read contigs compressed into a long-read backbone.
pipelines.description: DBG2OLC uses short-read contigs to compact long-read overlaps and assemble large genomes with reduced correction cost.

pipelines.label: EukCC eukaryotic MAG QC
pipelines.tagline: Evaluate eukaryotic MAG completeness with eukaryote-aware markers.
pipelines.description: EukCC evaluates metagenome-recovered eukaryotic genomes with lineage-aware marker sets so eukaryotic MAG quality can be estimated more honestly than with prokaryotic-only QC tools.

pipelines.label: FALCON diploid assembly
pipelines.tagline: Long-read unzipping for heterozygous diploid genomes.
pipelines.description: FALCON and FALCON-Unzip combine long-read correction, assembly, and haplotype-aware unzipping for phased diploid assemblies.

pipelines.label: Fast-Plast plastome recovery
pipelines.tagline: Chloroplast-focused recovery with IR validation and oriented plastome structure.
pipelines.description: Fast-Plast recruits chloroplast reads from genome-skimming data, resolves plastome structure, validates inverted-repeat boundaries, and outputs an oriented plastome assembly.

pipelines.label: FinisherSC long-read upgrade
pipelines.tagline: Upgrade contigs post-assembly with repeat-aware long-read evidence.
pipelines.description: FinisherSC upgrades draft contigs using long reads after the primary assembler is done, emphasizing repeat-aware merging and improved concordance.

pipelines.label: Flye on noisy long reads
pipelines.tagline: Repeat-graph noisy-read path.
pipelines.description: A noisy-long-read path defined by repeat-graph construction and repeat disambiguation.

pipelines.label: GetOrganelle organelle recovery
pipelines.tagline: Recruit organelle reads from genome skims and disentangle the assembly graph.
pipelines.description: GetOrganelle recovers plastid and mitochondrial genomes by recruiting target reads from broader datasets and resolving the resulting organelle graph.

pipelines.label: GFAffix graph cleanup
pipelines.tagline: Collapse redundant affixes in an induced pangenome graph.
pipelines.description: GFAffix removes redundant walk-preserving affixes from a pangenome graph and is typically used after graph induction workflows such as PGGB.

pipelines.label: GTDB-Tk MAG taxonomy placement
pipelines.tagline: Place recovered genomes into a standardized phylogenomic taxonomy.
pipelines.description: GTDB-Tk classifies recovered genomes against the Genome Taxonomy Database so MAGs can be compared and reported under a consistent phylogenomic naming framework.

pipelines.label: HiCanu on HiFi
pipelines.tagline: Homopolymer-compressed overlap path.
pipelines.description: A HiFi overlap path centered on homopolymer compression before difficult repeat handling.

pipelines.label: hifiasm with Hi-C / trio
pipelines.tagline: Long-range phasing augmentation.
pipelines.description: The same core HiFi hifiasm path but with explicit long-range phasing modules driven by orthogonal support data.

pipelines.label: hifiasm on HiFi
pipelines.tagline: Phased-graph HiFi path.
pipelines.description: A path centered on phased assembly graphs and haplotype partitioning for diploid HiFi assembly.

pipelines.label: hifiasm-ONT on ONT simplex
pipelines.tagline: ONT-specialized graph cleaning path.
pipelines.description: An ONT-first path centered on ONT-aware graph construction and cleanup.

pipelines.label: hifiasm-UL on HiFi + ONT ultra-long
pipelines.tagline: Double-graph bridging path.
pipelines.description: A double-graph hybrid path centered on ultra-long bridging for highly complete assemblies.

pipelines.label: Homopolish finishing
pipelines.tagline: Use homologous genomes to remove systematic ONT errors.
pipelines.description: Homopolish uses related genomes to identify and correct systematic polishing errors in nanopore assemblies.

pipelines.label: IDBA-UD uneven-depth single-cell assembly
pipelines.tagline: Use depth-relative filtering and local rescue for highly nonuniform single-cell data.
pipelines.description: IDBA-UD tackles extreme depth variability with multi-threshold filtering and local assembly logic, making it a foundational uneven-depth single-cell assembler.

pipelines.label: Juicer contact-map generation
pipelines.tagline: Hi-C reads to normalized assembly contact maps.
pipelines.description: Juicer converts Hi-C reads into normalized multi-resolution contact maps that downstream scaffolders and curators consume.

pipelines.label: LACHESIS chromosome scaffolding
pipelines.tagline: Early Hi-C clustering, ordering, and orienting.
pipelines.description: LACHESIS is an early Hi-C scaffolder that groups, orders, and orients scaffolds into chromosomes.

pipelines.label: LAI repeat-space validation
pipelines.tagline: Repeat completeness measured through intact LTR structure.
pipelines.description: A repeat-focused QC path that scores assembly quality in repeat-dense genomes through intact LTR retrotransposon recovery and LAI.

pipelines.label: LINKS alignment-free scaffolding
pipelines.tagline: Scaffold drafts from noisy long-read k-mer pairs.
pipelines.description: LINKS uses long-read k-mer pairs to scaffold assemblies without full read alignment or base correction.

pipelines.label: LJA on HiFi
pipelines.tagline: Variable-k HiFi path.
pipelines.description: A HiFi path that emphasizes multiplex de Bruijn structure and variable-k refinement.

pipelines.label: Loblolly pine v2 mega-reads upgrade
pipelines.tagline: PacBio-assisted mega-read upgrade path.
pipelines.description: A giant conifer upgrade path that adds PacBio long reads to a short-read base through MaSuRCA mega-reads. This improves contiguity, but it remains an HPC-class workflow rather than a local one.

pipelines.label: Loblolly pine v1 short-read megagenome
pipelines.tagline: Super-read conifer baseline path.
pipelines.description: The original 22-Gb loblolly pine reference path built around haploid tissue, clone resources, and MaSuRCA super-reads. It is informative historically, but still far beyond normal local hardware.

pipelines.label: L_RNA_scaffolder transcript scaffolding
pipelines.tagline: Use transcript alignments to improve scaffold continuity in fragmented drafts.
pipelines.description: L_RNA_scaffolder uses transcript alignments to bridge contigs, with a demonstrated use case in a highly polymorphic pearl oyster draft genome.

pipelines.label: LRScaf draft scaffolding
pipelines.tagline: Scale long-read scaffolding to larger genomes.
pipelines.description: LRScaf uses long noisy reads to improve fragmented drafts and was benchmarked across genomes ranging from bacteria to human and maize.

pipelines.label: MaxBin 2 marker-guided binning
pipelines.tagline: Recover MAGs with abundance profiles and lineage markers.
pipelines.description: MaxBin 2 seeds bins with lineage markers and refines them using abundance and composition evidence across one or more metagenomic datasets.

pipelines.label: Medaka ONT polishing
pipelines.tagline: Neural polishing from basecalled ONT reads.
pipelines.description: Medaka polishes draft assemblies with ONT reads using a neural pileup model.

pipelines.label: MeDuSa multi-draft scaffolding
pipelines.tagline: Scaffold a draft genome with multiple related drafts or references.
pipelines.description: MeDuSa formalizes scaffolding with multiple related genomes as a graph problem and builds scaffolds without relying on paired-end libraries.

pipelines.label: MEGAHIT short-read metagenome assembly
pipelines.tagline: Assemble large microbial communities with a succinct de Bruijn graph.
pipelines.description: MEGAHIT targets large and complex metagenomic short-read datasets with memory-efficient succinct graph construction.

pipelines.label: Merqury reference-free validation
pipelines.tagline: k-mer spectra for QV, completeness, and phase quality.
pipelines.description: A reference-free QC path that uses accurate read k-mers to estimate consensus quality, completeness, copy balance, and phasing behavior.

pipelines.label: MetaBAT 2 MAG binning
pipelines.tagline: Adaptive composition-and-coverage binning for draft MAG recovery.
pipelines.description: MetaBAT 2 partitions assembled metagenome contigs into genome bins using composition and abundance cues, then hands the result to downstream MAG QC.

pipelines.label: MetaCarvel graph-motif metascaffolding
pipelines.tagline: Use graph motifs to scaffold metagenome contigs and interpret variation.
pipelines.description: MetaCarvel analyzes assembly graph motifs in metagenome contigs to improve scaffolding while exposing signals of biological variation and problematic joins.

pipelines.label: metaFlye long-read metagenome assembly
pipelines.tagline: Extend repeat-graph assembly to uneven long-read microbial communities.
pipelines.description: metaFlye applies long-read repeat-graph assembly to real metagenomes with uneven abundance and intra-species heterogeneity.

pipelines.label: MetaQUAST metagenome benchmarking
pipelines.tagline: Assess mixed-community assemblies against multiple close references.
pipelines.description: MetaQUAST extends reference-based assembly evaluation to metagenomes, adding interspecies misassembly reporting and multi-reference summaries.

pipelines.label: metaSPAdes metagenome assembly
pipelines.tagline: Handle strain-rich microbial communities with graph simplification tuned for microdiversity.
pipelines.description: metaSPAdes adapts SPAdes ideas to metagenomic short reads and emphasizes robust handling of strain variation.

pipelines.label: Metassembler reconciliation
pipelines.tagline: Merge complementary draft assemblies.
pipelines.description: Metassembler compares multiple draft assemblies and reconciles them into a single improved result.

pipelines.label: Metaviral SPAdes viral recovery
pipelines.tagline: Extract viral genomes from metagenomic assembly graphs.
pipelines.description: Metaviral SPAdes searches metagenomic assembly graphs for viral subgraphs and emits candidate viral genomes or contigs rather than relying only on flat contig classification.

pipelines.label: miniasm + Racon
pipelines.tagline: Fast draft assembly followed by long-read consensus.
pipelines.description: Miniasm skips pre-correction to build a rapid draft, then Racon refines consensus from long-read alignments.

pipelines.label: Minigraph-Cactus pangenome
pipelines.tagline: Build an assembly-derived graph reference from phased genomes.
pipelines.description: Minigraph and Minigraph-Cactus construct a pangenome graph directly from high-quality assemblies and preserve complex homology relationships.

pipelines.label: MitoZ animal mitogenome workflow
pipelines.tagline: Recover, annotate, and package mitochondrial genomes from short-read data.
pipelines.description: MitoZ couples mitochondrial recovery with annotation and visualization, making it a fuller mitogenome packaging workflow than a bare assembler alone.

pipelines.label: Nanopolish signal-level finishing
pipelines.tagline: Use raw ONT signal to refine consensus.
pipelines.description: Nanopolish improves draft assemblies with signal-level ONT evidence and was a central finisher in early ONT-only assembly workflows.

pipelines.label: NextPolish finishing
pipelines.tagline: Stepwise short-read and long-read consensus refinement.
pipelines.description: NextPolish applies short-read and long-read polishing modules to improve assembly consensus after long-read assembly.

pipelines.label: Norway spruce megagenome
pipelines.tagline: Short-read plus clone-pool conifer path.
pipelines.description: A giant conifer workflow built from short-read WGS, pooled-clone assembly, and assembly merging. This is a clearly non-local, cluster-grade assembly strategy.

pipelines.label: NOVOPlasty seed-and-extend organelle assembly
pipelines.tagline: Start from a conserved seed and walk to a small circular organelle genome.
pipelines.description: NOVOPlasty uses a seed sequence to pull out organelle reads from general short-read data and extend them into a recovered circular target.

pipelines.label: npScarf real-time finishing
pipelines.tagline: Stream ONT reads into a live scaffolding run.
pipelines.description: npScarf updates draft scaffolds in real time as nanopore reads stream off the instrument and can recover complete microbial replicons.

pipelines.label: ntEdit k-mer polishing
pipelines.tagline: Bloom-filter polishing with short-read evidence.
pipelines.description: ntEdit uses memory-efficient Bloom-filter k-mers to polish assemblies from accurate short-read evidence.

pipelines.label: odgi pangenome analytics
pipelines.tagline: Analyze graph paths after pangenome induction.
pipelines.description: odgi supports dynamic analysis, layout, and interpretation of pangenome graphs after induction by tools like PGGB or Minigraph-Cactus.

pipelines.label: OPERA-LG repeat-aware scaffolding
pipelines.tagline: Exact scaffolding for repeat-rich draft assemblies.
pipelines.description: OPERA-LG performs repeat-aware exact scaffolding for large, repeat-rich eukaryotic genomes with performance guarantees.

pipelines.label: OPERA-MS hybrid metagenome assembly
pipelines.tagline: Cluster mixed-community assemblies and scaffold them with long reads.
pipelines.description: OPERA-MS combines short-read and long-read evidence to partition metagenome assemblies into more coherent clusters and then scaffold them exactly, improving continuity and rare-species recovery.

pipelines.label: Optical-map scaffold correction
pipelines.tagline: Use Bionano evidence to repair scaffolding artifacts.
pipelines.description: A structural-correction path that uses optical maps to validate scaffold organization, fuse overlaps, and reduce downstream misassemblies.

pipelines.label: PEPPER-Margin-DeepVariant polishing
pipelines.tagline: Haplotype-aware long-read polishing and phasing.
pipelines.description: PEPPER-Margin-DeepVariant brings haplotype-aware long-read interpretation to polishing and phasing, especially in difficult diploid regions.

pipelines.label: PGGB plus vg pangenome workflow
pipelines.tagline: Reference-free graph induction with downstream graph indexing.
pipelines.description: PGGB builds an unbiased pangenome graph from multiple assemblies, while vg indexes and queries the resulting variation graph for downstream analysis.

pipelines.label: purge_dups redundancy cleanup
pipelines.tagline: Remove false duplicate haplotigs from a primary assembly.
pipelines.description: A heterozygosity-cleanup path that uses read depth and overlap evidence to purge redundant allelic contigs from a primary assembly.

pipelines.label: Purge Haplotigs cleanup
pipelines.tagline: Coverage-aware reassignment of allelic contigs.
pipelines.description: Purge Haplotigs uses coverage and alignment profiles to identify and reassign haplotigs, reducing false duplication in diploid assemblies.

pipelines.label: QUAST structural assessment
pipelines.tagline: Reference-alignment view of contiguity versus correctness.
pipelines.description: A structural-QC path that compares an assembly to a benchmark reference to report misassemblies, NA50, and alignment breakpoints.

pipelines.label: Ragout multi-reference scaffolding
pipelines.tagline: Use multiple related genomes to order bacterial draft contigs.
pipelines.description: Ragout combines multiple references, evolutionary structure, and synteny blocks to produce high-quality bacterial scaffolds.

pipelines.label: RagTag reference-guided patching
pipelines.tagline: Reference-guided scaffolding plus patching.
pipelines.description: RagTag orders contigs against a related reference and can patch or correct assemblies with homology-guided evidence.

pipelines.label: Rascaf exon-block scaffolding
pipelines.tagline: Use exon-block paths to add transcript-consistent scaffold joins.
pipelines.description: Rascaf leverages intron-spanning RNA-seq evidence and an exon-block graph to recover contig joins and improve gene models in draft plant assemblies.

pipelines.label: Redundans heterozygous cleanup
pipelines.tagline: Remove redundant allelic contigs before further finishing.
pipelines.description: Redundans targets highly heterozygous draft assemblies by identifying redundant alternative contigs and producing a cleaner primary representation.

pipelines.label: single-cell SPAdes mini-metagenome assembly
pipelines.tagline: Carry single-cell-aware graph handling into pooled low-complexity cell sets.
pipelines.description: The same chimeric-MDA-aware SPAdes logic extends to mini-metagenomes, a lower-complexity pooled-cell regime that sits between single-cell and full metagenomic assembly.

pipelines.label: Sealer gap closing
pipelines.tagline: Bloom-filter graph gap closure for large genomes.
pipelines.description: Sealer closes scaffold gaps by traversing Bloom-filter de Bruijn graphs and was explicitly demonstrated on human and white spruce drafts.

pipelines.label: SemiBin2 long-read MAG recovery
pipelines.tagline: Self-supervised binning for short- and long-read metagenomes.
pipelines.description: SemiBin2 uses self-supervised learning to recover better MAGs from both short-read and long-read metagenomes, including noisy long-read settings.

pipelines.label: seqwish plus smoothxg pangenome induction
pipelines.tagline: Induce a pangenome graph from alignments and smooth it for downstream use.
pipelines.description: seqwish converts pairwise alignments between assemblies into an unbiased pangenome graph, while smoothxg simplifies and linearizes the resulting variation graph for downstream analysis.

pipelines.label: Shasta on ONT
pipelines.tagline: Marker-compression ONT path.
pipelines.description: A speed-oriented ONT path defined by marker compression before graph construction.

pipelines.label: single-cell SPAdes assembly
pipelines.tagline: Assemble MDA-amplified single cells with chimeric-edge-aware graph logic.
pipelines.description: single-cell SPAdes adapts de Bruijn graph assembly to the extreme coverage bias and chimeric artifacts introduced by multiple displacement amplification.

pipelines.label: SSPACE-LongRead scaffolding
pipelines.tagline: PacBio-backed iterative long-read scaffolding.
pipelines.description: SSPACE-LongRead iteratively scaffolds draft contigs using PacBio long reads and was designed around incomplete bacterial drafts.

pipelines.label: Sugar pine megagenome
pipelines.tagline: White pine super-read megagenome path.
pipelines.description: A 31-Gb sugar pine path that combines haploid sampling, clone resources, and MaSuRCA-style read compression. This is another clearly cluster-scale conifer workflow.

pipelines.label: TGS-GapCloser finishing
pipelines.tagline: Use long reads to bridge scaffold gaps.
pipelines.description: TGS-GapCloser uses long reads to bridge gaps in large-genome scaffolds without requiring very high long-read coverage.

pipelines.label: Tigmint plus ARCS linked-read curation
pipelines.tagline: Correct draft misassemblies before linked-read scaffolding.
pipelines.description: Tigmint cuts draft assemblies at poorly supported linked-read regions, after which ARCS scaffolds the corrected fragments into more contiguous results.

pipelines.label: Trio binning for heterozygous diploids
pipelines.tagline: Parental markers split long reads before assembly.
pipelines.description: A heterozygosity-focused path in which parental short reads partition offspring long reads before separate haplotype assembly with TrioCanu.

pipelines.label: Unicycler hybrid bacterial assembly
pipelines.tagline: Resolve short-read graphs with long-read bridges.
pipelines.description: Unicycler starts from a short-read assembly graph and uses long reads to resolve and finish bacterial genomes into complete replicons.

pipelines.label: VAMB latent-space binning
pipelines.tagline: Use variational autoencoders to recover higher-quality MAGs.
pipelines.description: VAMB integrates sequence composition and coabundance information in a variational latent space to recover more complete bins and better separate closely related strains.

pipelines.label: Verkko on HiFi + ONT ultra-long
pipelines.tagline: Hybrid bridging path.
pipelines.description: A hybrid path that combines multiplex de Bruijn structure with ultra-long bridging and long-range phasing support.

pipelines.label: VirSorter2 viral recovery
pipelines.tagline: Detect diverse viral contigs with hallmark-aware classification.
pipelines.description: VirSorter2 screens candidate contigs with an expert-guided multi-classifier architecture to recover diverse DNA and RNA viral sequence from metagenomic assemblies.

pipelines.label: wtdbg2 long-read assembly
pipelines.tagline: Fuzzy de Bruijn graph assembly for noisy long reads.
pipelines.description: wtdbg2 builds contigs from noisy long reads with a fuzzy de Bruijn graph and lightweight consensus.

pipelines.label: YaHS chromosome scaffolding
pipelines.tagline: Hi-C links for chromosome-scale scaffolds.
pipelines.description: YaHS uses Hi-C contact signals to order and orient contigs into chromosome-scale scaffolds.

pipelines.label: hifiasm-meta HiFi metagenome assembly
pipelines.tagline: Assemble HiFi metagenomes with strain-aware graph cleaning and circular-genome recovery.
pipelines.description: hifiasm-meta extends hifiasm for HiFi metagenomes by protecting low-abundance strains, pruning overlaps with coverage information, and recovering many complete circular genomes directly from complex communities.

pipelines.label: metaMDBG HiFi metagenome assembly
pipelines.tagline: Assemble accurate long-read metagenomes in minimizer space.
pipelines.description: metaMDBG builds a minimizer-space de Bruijn graph with abundance-aware filtering to recover more circular genomes and better long-read metagenome assemblies from PacBio HiFi data.

pipelines.label: VIBRANT viral curation
pipelines.tagline: Recover, annotate, and quality-rank viral contigs with annotation-guided curation.
pipelines.description: VIBRANT uses protein-signature annotation, learned viral scoring, and post-classification curation to recover viral contigs, estimate their quality, and highlight viral community function.

pipelines.label: Phables phage graph resolution
pipelines.tagline: Resolve high-quality phage genomes from fragmented assembly graphs.
pipelines.description: Phables extracts and resolves bacteriophage genomes from metagenome assembly-graph components, improving recovery beyond flat contig classification alone.

pipelines.label: geNomad virus and provirus classification
pipelines.tagline: Classify mobile elements and detect proviruses at metagenome scale.
pipelines.description: geNomad combines marker models and neural sequence classification to recover viral contigs, detect proviruses, and annotate mobile genetic elements.

pipelines.label: iPHoP viral host prediction
pipelines.tagline: Link uncultivated viral genomes to likely bacterial or archaeal hosts.
pipelines.description: iPHoP integrates multiple signals to maximize reliable host prediction for metagenome-derived viral genomes while controlling false discovery rate.

pipelines.label: PASA plus EVidenceModeler annotation update
pipelines.tagline: Refine gene models with transcript assemblies and evidence-weighted consensus.
pipelines.description: PASA assembles transcript alignments into improved gene structures, while EVidenceModeler combines transcript, protein, and ab initio evidence into a consensus annotation.

pipelines.label: Velvet-SC single-cell assembly
pipelines.tagline: Retain low-coverage paths in single-cell de Bruijn graphs.
pipelines.description: Velvet-SC modifies de Bruijn graph pruning to preserve real low-coverage sequence in highly uneven single-cell datasets.

pipelines.label: HyDA colored-graph co-assembly
pipelines.tagline: Co-assemble related single cells with colored graph labels.
pipelines.description: HyDA uses colored de Bruijn graphs to combine related single-cell libraries while preserving source-specific graph evidence.

pipelines.label: EukRep eukaryotic contig recovery
pipelines.tagline: Separate eukaryotic contigs from mixed metagenome assemblies.
pipelines.description: EukRep classifies assembled metagenome contigs as eukaryotic, enabling downstream eukaryotic genome recovery and annotation.

pipelines.label: MetaEuk eukaryotic gene discovery
pipelines.tagline: Discover genes on fragmented eukaryotic metagenome contigs at scale.
pipelines.description: MetaEuk performs sensitive translated-search-based gene discovery on eukaryotic metagenome contigs using large protein reference databases.

pipelines.label: binny iterative MAG refinement
pipelines.tagline: Refine bins with iterative clustering and marker-based evaluation.
pipelines.description: binny iteratively clusters contigs with coverage and k-mer features, then accepts refined MAGs using lineage-marker completeness and purity checks.

pipelines.label: GraphAligner graph inspection
pipelines.tagline: Align long reads directly to sequence graphs for curation and debugging.
pipelines.description: GraphAligner maps long reads to graph structures to inspect tangled regions, validate graph traversals, and support graph-aware debugging.

pipelines.label: Dorado neural basecalling
pipelines.tagline: Decode raw ONT signal with neural simplex and duplex models.
pipelines.description: Dorado turns raw nanopore signal into basecalled reads with neural models and supports duplex decoding for higher-accuracy output.

pipelines.label: DeepConsensus HiFi correction
pipelines.tagline: Correct PacBio CCS reads with a gap-aware transformer.
pipelines.description: DeepConsensus applies gap-aware transformer models to PacBio CCS/HiFi data, improving read quality before downstream assembly and variant calling.

pipelines.label: DeepVariant long-read variant calling
pipelines.tagline: Call long-read variants from pileup-image tensors.
pipelines.description: DeepVariant uses learned pileup-image representations of alignments to call variants and is widely used for HG002-style long-read benchmarking and QC.

pipelines.label: Clair3 hybrid long-read variant calling
pipelines.tagline: Resolve hard long-read candidates with pileup plus full alignment.
pipelines.description: Clair3 combines fast pileup calling with a neural full-alignment model for difficult long-read variant candidates.

pipelines.label: DeepVirFinder CNN viral recovery
pipelines.tagline: Score viral contigs directly from sequence with deep learning.
pipelines.description: DeepVirFinder uses convolutional neural networks to detect viral sequences directly from metagenomic contigs without relying only on marker genes.

pipelines.label: Tiara deep eukaryotic contig classification
pipelines.tagline: Use deep learning to separate eukaryotic and organellar contigs.
pipelines.description: Tiara uses staged neural models to classify eukaryotic and organellar contigs from mixed metagenome assemblies.
